canonical_question: How do error handling, rate limit headers, and retry-after patterns differ across ERPs?
aliases: ERP API error response format comparison
Salesforce vs SAP vs Oracle vs NetSuite error handling comparison
ERP integration retry logic best practices cross-platform

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: 2025-10 -- Salesforce API v62.0; Dynamics 365 F&O 10.0.36 made user-based limits optional

constraints: Salesforce returns Retry-After header on 429 but NOT on governor limit errors
SAP S/4HANA Cloud has no standardized rate limit headers without API Management
Oracle Fusion returns 429 but Retry-After header is not guaranteed
NetSuite returns 429 for REST but 403 for SOAP concurrency violations
Workday does not publicly document error response schemas
Acumatica returns HTTP 500 (not 429) when request queue is full
IFS Cloud returns OData-style errors but does not implement standard rate limit headers

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs rate limit values and quotas, not error formats
User needs dead letter queue architecture patterns

related_to.label: ERP rate limits and throttling comparison

solves.label: Dead letter queue patterns for ERP integrations

often_confused_with.label: Rate limits card covers quotas; this card covers error response formats and retry logic

inputs_needed.question: What integration pattern do you need?
